goals: Initialise a Mastra agent on the backend with a Narrator system prompt defining its RPG storyteller role.
Provide a `generate_image` tool the agent can call autonomously when it needs to illustrate a scene.
Expose `POST /chat` so a caller can send a player message and receive a full narrative response.
Expose `WebSocket /ws` so response tokens are streamed to the client in real time, with the
Ship integration tests (Media Service mocked) that can be run with `bun test` to confirm

userStories.title: Mastra agent initialised with Narrator system prompt
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Mastra agent registered on the ElysiaJS backend with a Narrator system prompt so that all subsequent player interactions are handled by a character-consistent RPG storyteller.

acceptanceCriteria.text: A Mastra `Agent` instance is created in `apps/backend` with an `id` of `"narrator"`.
The system prompt defines the agent as an imaginative RPG narrator that builds the world
The agent is accessible as a singleton (e.g. exported from `src/agent/narrator.ts`).
The backend starts cleanly with `bun run dev` and logs confirm the agent is registered.
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: `generate_image` tool wired to Media Service
userStories.description: As a Mastra agent, I want a `generate_image` tool I can call with a text prompt so that I can request scene images from the Media Service without hard-coding HTTP logic inside the agent.

acceptanceCriteria.text: A Mastra tool named `generate_image` is defined and registered on the Narrator agent.
The tool accepts a single string parameter `prompt`.
On invocation it calls `POST /generate/image` on the Media Service (URL from env var
It polls `GET /jobs/{job_id}` at a configurable interval (default 2 s) until the job
On `"done"` it returns the `image_b64` string to the agent.
On `"failed"` it returns a structured error string: `"Image generation failed: <error message>"`.
Poll timeout defaults to 120 s; exceeded polls return the same structured error.
The tool is created via `createGenerateImageTool(client: MediaServiceClient)` factory;
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: `POST /chat` REST endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a `POST /chat` endpoint that runs a player message through the Narrator agent and returns the full narrative response so that I can verify agent behaviour without a WebSocket client.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `POST /chat` accepts JSON body `{ "message": "<player message>" }`.
Response is HTTP 200 with body `{ "response": "<narrative text>", "images": ["<b64 or url>", …] }`.
Missing or empty `message` returns HTTP 400 with `{ "error": "message is required" }`.
The endpoint calls the Narrator agent and awaits the full response before replying.
If the agent invokes `generate_image`, the resulting `image_b64` is included in `images`.
Errors from the agent (including tool failures) return HTTP 500 with
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: WebSocket `/ws` streaming endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer (and future frontend), I want a WebSocket endpoint that streams Narrator agent tokens as they are generated so that the UI can show text appearing progressively and display the image at the end.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Connecting to `ws://localhost:<BACKEND_PORT>/ws` establishes a WebSocket session.
Sending a JSON frame `{ "message": "<player message>" }` starts agent inference.
Each text token is sent as a frame `{ "type": "token", "content": "<token>" }`.
When a `generate_image` tool call completes, a frame
When the full response is complete, a frame `{ "type": "done" }` is sent.
If an error occurs, a frame `{ "type": "error", "message": "<description>" }` is sent
Frames are valid JSON; malformed incoming frames are replied to with an `"error"` frame
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: Integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests covering the chat endpoint, WebSocket, and tool flow (Media Service mocked) so that I can confirm correctness before building the frontend.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Tests live in `apps/backend/src/__tests__/` and run with `bun test`.
`test_chat_endpoint.test.ts`: verifies HTTP 200 response with narrative text; verifies
`test_ws_endpoint.test.ts`: verifies token frames are received; verifies `"done"` frame
`test_generate_image_tool.test.ts`: verifies the tool polls until `"done"`, returns
The Media Service is mocked via a configurable HTTP client or test double — no real
All tests pass with `bun test` from the repo root.
Typecheck / lint passes.

functionalRequirements.description: ** Mastra `Agent` with id `"narrator"` initialised at backend startup; system prompt
** Mastra tool `generate_image(prompt: string)` registered on the Narrator agent;
** `POST /chat` → HTTP 200 `{ response: string, images: string[] }` or
** `WS /ws` frame protocol: `token | image | done | error` frame types; keeps
** Media Service base URL configured via env var `MEDIA_SERVICE_URL`
** `generate_image` tool uses a poll interval of 2 s and a max wait of 120 s.
** The `generate_image` tool must be created via a factory function that receives a
** No `console.log` in production code paths; use structured logging (ElysiaJS logger
** Agent module lives in `apps/backend/src/agent/`; transport handlers (REST + WS)
** All tests run via `bun test`; no extra test runner required.
